stories.title: data_quality column too narrow for 'attribution_anomaly' (VARCHAR(16)<19) -- chain-blocking
acceptance: v0012 migration MODIFY both columns to VARCHAR(20), idempotent
test asserting column width >= max CHECK-permitted value length
conditionalOutcomes: if other CHECK-enum columns have the same width mismatch, fold the audit in
stories.goal: Widen drive_summary + drive_statistics data_quality columns so the tripwire can stamp 'attribution_anomaly' (unblocks US-364).
stories.completionNotes: SERVER-ONLY. TDD RED->GREEN. (1) ORM: new SSOT constant DATA_QUALITY_COLUMN_LENGTH=20 (models.py); both data_quality columns String(16)->String(DATA_QUALITY_COLUMN_LENGTH) on DriveSummary + DriveStatistic; constant exported in __all__. (2) v0012 migration (FORWARD-ONLY; v0009/v0010 UNTOUCHED): MODIFY both data_quality columns to VARCHAR(20) NOT NULL DEFAULT 'full' (full def re-stated so the widen preserves NOT NULL + DEFAULT). Idempotent via a CHARACTER_MAXIMUM_LENGTH probe per table (width>=20 -> no-op; covers fresh create_all from the VARCHAR(20) ORM + re-run + already-wider); post-condition width re-probe raises SchemaProbeError on the silent-no-op class; table-missing + column-missing + MODIFY-failure all raise MigrationError. Registered _V0012 last in ALL_MIGRATIONS (+ registry docstring history line). (3) Width-INVARIANT guard test asserting every data_quality column >= longest CHECK-permitted value (the regression guard); PLUS a generic audit over EVERY CHECK 'col IN (...)' enum column across all ORM tables (conditionalOutcome: capture_method VARCHAR(32)>=15 confirmed fine -- only the two data_quality cols had the mismatch). Forward-only convention forced updating 3 coupled tests (NOT the shipped migrations): v0009 + v0010 DDL-parity tests now pin their FROZEN historical VARCHAR(16) (current-width ORM parity moved to the v0012 width-invariant guard); v0011 test_v0011RegisteredLast -> test_v0011RegisteredBeforeV0012 (durable sorted-tail form). GATES: pytest tests/server -m 'not slow' GREEN (0 failed); ruff clean on all 7 files. IRL-ONLY DEFERRED to the V0.28.2 deploy drill (validation.bigDefinitionOfDone; no MariaDB/prod on dev): SELECT column_type post-v0012 -> both varchar(20); recompute_drive_analytics --drive-id 23/24 -> attribution_anomaly no DataError; --drive-id 25 -> full; idempotent re-run; releases F-005+F-007 regression_manifest HOLD (closes US-364 + chain-merge pre-condition #4). Changes UNSTAGED (PM owns commits).

validation.validationMethod: V0.28.2 patch (drill-revealed regression per PM Rule 9): widen drive_summary + drive_statistics data_quality VARCHAR(16)->VARCHAR(20) via v0012 so recompute_drive_analytics can stamp attribution_anomaly. On deploy: DESCRIBE shows varchar(20); recompute drives 23+24 -> attribution_anomaly, drive 25 -> full, idempotent re-run; releases F-005 + F-007 regression_manifest HOLD (closes US-364 + chain-merge pre-condition #4).
